name: project-development
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "start an LLM project", "design batch pipeline", "evaluate task-model fit", "structure agent project", or mentions pipeline architecture, agent-assisted development, cost estimation, or choosing between LLM and traditional approaches.
capabilities: Evaluate whether a task is suited for LLM processing versus traditional code
Design staged pipelines with acquire, prepare, process, parse, and render stages
Implement file-system-based state management for idempotent processing
Estimate token costs and processing time before execution
Structure prompts for reliable parsing of LLM outputs
Choose between single-agent and multi-agent architectures
author: ChakshuGautam
securitySummary: This is a documentation and educational skill teaching LLM pipeline development methodology. All 184 static findings are false positives: 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are words like 'MD' (markdown) in documentation, 'external_commands' detections are markdown code blocks with backticks, and filesystem operations are legitimate pipeline state management patterns. No malicious behavior, network exfiltration, or credential access present.
valueStatement: Building LLM applications without a clear methodology leads to wasted time and budget overruns. This skill provides a proven framework for evaluating task fit, architecting pipelines, and iterating efficiently.
userTitle: Design and Build LLM Batch Processing Pipelines
limitations: Does not provide ready-to-use API integrations for specific LLM providers
Cost estimates are approximations that vary by model and usage
Multi-agent patterns require additional specialized skills for full implementation
Template code requires customization for specific use cases

useCases.title: Build a Content Analysis Pipeline
useCases.description: Create a batch processing system that analyzes hundreds of documents with structured LLM outputs and reliable parsing.

useCases.title: Evaluate LLM Project Feasibility
useCases.description: Assess whether a proposed feature is suited for LLM processing and estimate development costs before committing resources.

useCases.title: Design Scalable Processing Architecture
useCases.description: Structure a parallel processing pipeline with checkpointing, error handling, and cost-effective token management.

promptTemplates.title: Task Fit Evaluation
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new project that might use LLMs
promptTemplates.prompt: Evaluate whether this task is suited for LLM processing: [describe the task, inputs, and expected outputs]. Consider synthesis requirements, error tolerance, and accuracy needs.

promptTemplates.title: Pipeline Architecture Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a pipeline architecture for processing [number] items of [type]. Include stage breakdown, file structure, and parallelization strategy.

promptTemplates.title: Cost Estimation
promptTemplates.prompt: Estimate the cost to process [number] items with [model name]. Each item has approximately [X] words of input and requires [Y] words of output.

promptTemplates.title: Prompt Structure Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a prompt template that produces parseable output with these fields: [list fields]. Include format specification and parsing strategy for handling variations.

bestPractices: Always validate task-model fit with a manual prototype before building automation
Use the file system for state management to enable easy debugging and idempotent processing
Start with minimal architecture and add complexity only when proven necessary
antiPatterns: Building automation before testing if the model can perform the task manually
Combining all pipeline stages into one script without intermediate outputs
Adding excessive guardrails that constrain model capabilities unnecessarily

faq.question: When should I use a single-agent versus multi-agent architecture?
faq.answer: Use multi-agent when tasks exceed single context window limits or require parallel exploration. Single-agent works better for independent batch items.

faq.question: How do I estimate costs before running a pipeline?
faq.answer: Count input tokens per item, estimate output tokens, multiply by item count, then add 20-30% buffer for retries. Use the cost estimation patterns provided.

faq.question: What makes a task unsuited for LLM processing?
faq.answer: Tasks requiring precise computation, real-time responses, perfect accuracy, or deterministic outputs are poor fits. Test manually first to verify.

faq.question: How do I handle LLM output parsing failures?
faq.answer: Build flexible regex parsers that handle format variations. Log failures for review rather than crashing. Provide sensible defaults for missing sections.

faq.question: Should I use specialized tools or simple primitives?
faq.answer: Start with minimal tools like bash and file system access. Add specialized tools only when they demonstrably improve results. More tools often means worse performance.

faq.question: How do I structure prompts for reliable parsing?
faq.answer: Include explicit format specifications with examples, use section markers like headers, and tell the model you will parse programmatically.
